data.title: PE firm Warburg weighs buyout of financial services firm JTC, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Private equity firm Warburg is exploring a potential acquisition of JTC Plc in a possible challenge to rival Permira's pursuit of the London-listed financial services firm, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

data.title: REG - Global Smlr Cos.Tst - Loan Facility
data.description:
RNS Number : 1948Z Global Smaller Cos. Trust PLC (The) 12 September 2025  

London Stock Exchange Announcement

The Global Smaller Companies Trust plc

(the 'Company')

LEI: 2138008RRULYQP8VP386

Multicurrency revolving loan facility

The Company advises that it has entered into a one-year unsecured multicurrency revolving loan facility of up to £35,000,000 with The Royal Bank of Scotland International Limited (London Branch) (the 'Facility').

This replaces the Company's existing unsecured multicurrency revolving loan facility of up to £35,000,000 with The Royal Bank of Scotland International Limited (London Branch) which matures on 13 September 2025. 

The Company has entered into this Facility for the purpose of pursuing its investment objective and for general working capital requirements.

12 September 2025

For further information, please contact:

Ian Ridge

Columbia Threadneedle Investment Business Limited

Telephone: 020 7464 5000

data.title: J M Smucker slips after Argus downgrades to 'hold'
data.description:
** Shares of Uncrustables maker J.M. Smucker down 1.3% at $109.45 premarket

** Brokerage Argus Research downgrades SJM to "hold" from "buy" citing the co's struggles to grow volumes

** Analysts say tariffs are adding to margin challenges, raising concerns over profitability in the coffee segment

** Also adds, SJM faces ongoing commodity price risk due to its dependence on agricultural inputs vulnerable to external factors like weather and geopolitical instability

** Analysts say they may revisit "buy" rating if margin and volume growth show sustained improvement

** Up to last close, SJM stock up almost 1% YTD

data.title: US Futures Cautious
data.description:
US futures hovered near the flatline on Friday, as traders paused after the three major indexes notched fresh record highs in the previous session.

A CPI report that came in roughly in line with expectations reinforced bets that the Federal Reserve has room to resume rate cuts this year, beginning with a likely 25-basis-point reduction in the fed funds rate next week.

Traders expect the equivalent of two to three quarter-point cuts by year-end, with some even wagering on a larger half-point move next week.

The day’s calendar is light, with only preliminary September estimates for the University of Michigan’s consumer sentiment survey on tap.

In premarket trading, megacap stocks traded mixed: Nvidia (0.2%), Microsoft (1.3%), Broadcom (0.4%), and Alphabet (0.4%) gained, while Amazon and Meta were little changed and Apple declined 0.5%.

Meanwhile, Super Micro Computer jumped more than 6% ahead of the opening bell after announcing the launch of its Nvidia Blackwell Ultra solutions.

data.title: Nigeria cuts crude oil losses to 16-year low
data.description:
Nigeria reduced its crude oil losses to 9,600 barrels per day by July, marking the lowest level since 2009, the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) said on Friday.

The figure represents a significant drop from the 102,900 bpd lost in 2021, the highest in more than two decades.

The NUPRC said the progress was thanks to collaboration with security agencies, private contractors and host communities, as well as regulatory reforms such as metering audits.

Nigeria, Africa's largest oil producer, has long had to contend with oil theft, pipeline vandalism and ageing infrastructure, which have eroded government revenue and discouraged foreign investment in the sector.

Oil legislation enacted in 2021 to overhaul the regulatory framework, improve transparency and attract capital into upstream and midstream operations, has helped enforcement and infrastructure protection, the commission said.

Daily production stood at 1.71 million barrels in July, comprising 1.507 million barrels of crude oil and 204,864 barrels of condensates.

In a separate development, Nigeria's midstream and downstream gas infrastructure fund has signed a preliminary agreement with Afreximbank on $500 million of funding over the next four years for investments in gas infrastructure, the government said on social media platform X.

Nigeria has been turning to gas as an alternative fuel after it scrapped a popular but costly subsidy on petrol. That move sent petrol pump prices higher, drawing criticism from motorists and from businesses that use petrol to generate their own power.

data.title: RH Is Maintained at Overweight by Barclays
PRES TRUMP Q&A/FOX: REPEATS, FED'S POWELL 'A YEAR LATE' IN LOWERING INTEREST RATES; RE LISA COOK, 'HAVE SOMEONE IN THERE SHOW SHOULDN'T BE THERE'
Canada Building Permits Drop 0.1% in July
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canadian building permits edged downward in July due to weakness in demand to build factories, warehouses, and public-sector buildings like hospitals and schools.

The total value of building permits in July fell 0.1% from the previous month to a seasonally adjusted 11.86 billion Canadian dollars, or the equivalent of $8.57 billion, Statistics Canada said Friday.

On a 12-month basis, permits fell 4.9%.

Market expectations for July were not immediately available.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Armani's $7 Billion Farewell: Is LVMH About to Snatch the Last Great Independent?
data.description:
Giorgio Armani may have waited until the very end, but his heirs have now been handed one of the most high-stakes M&A playbooks in fashion. The late designer, who passed away at 91 earlier this month, left behind a detailed will that sets up a potential landmark sale of Giorgio Armani SpA starting with a 15% stake, rising to a full-blown takeover in three years. The three shortlisted suitors? LVMH (LVMHF), L'Oreal, and EssilorLuxottica each with deep ties to the Armani ecosystem and more than enough firepower to absorb the 57 billion valuation suggested by Berenberg analysts. For an industry grappling with post-boom fatigue, this is the kind of consolidation catalyst that could shake things loose.

Among the three, LVMH may stand out as the most strategically aligned buyer. The French luxury giant has quietly built a footprint across Italy buying into Tod's, backing Moncler's founder, and establishing itself as a long-term operator rather than a short-term acquirer. Bernard Arnault, whose relationship with Armani dates back years, once floated a potential alliance with the designer. While L'Oreal and EssilorLuxottica already hold long-term fragrance and eyewear licenses with Armani both renewed well into the 2030s LVMH could offer something different: group-level synergies, patient capital, and a track record of brand elevation without dilution. Armani's longstanding insistence on independence makes this all the more noteworthy his final act was one of precision, not impulse.

But this is far from a done deal. L'Oreal and LVMH have kept quiet. EssilorLuxottica has simply said it will carefully consider the offer. And for now, Armani's foundation alongside family members and trusted lieutenant Leo Dell'Orco holds the reins. An IPO is still on the table, though current luxury-market headwinds may tilt the odds toward a strategic buyer. Either way, this isn't just about one brand. It's about the accelerating end of founder-led luxury, and the tightening grip of a few global empires that now define the top end of the pyramid. Investors should be watching closely. The ripple effects could go well beyond Milan.

data.title: REG - Celebrus Tech - Transfer from treasury and TVR
data.description:
RNS Number : 1941Z Celebrus Technologies PLC 12 September 2025  

Celebrus Technologies plc

Transfer from treasury

and TVR

Celebrus Technologies plc (AIM: CLBS, "the Group", "Celebrus"), the AIM-listed data solutions provider, announces the transfer from treasury today of 92,941 ordinary shares of 2p each ("Ordinary Shares") pursuant to the exercise of employee share options ("Share Options").

Following the above transaction the Company advises that it has 40,431,453 Ordinary Shares in issue, with 810,288 Ordinary Shares held in treasury. Consequently, the total voting rights of the Company is 39,621,165.

The above voting rights figure of 39,621,165 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

Enquiries

Celebrus Technologies plc

Bill Bruno, Chief Executive Officer

Ash Mehta, Chief Financial Officer

+44 (0) 1932 893333

investors@celebrus.com

Cavendish (Nominated Adviser & Broker)

Julian Blunt / Edward Whiley / Elysia Bough, Corporate Finance

Tim Redfern / Harriet Ward, Corporate Broking

+44 (0) 20 7220 0500

About Celebrus Technologies plc

Celebrus sets the gold standard globally for improving marketing effectiveness and preventing fraud across all industries. We are laser-focused on improving the relationships between brands and consumers via better data. This means innovating better ways to manage digital identity and know your consumers, even when they are not logged in. Celebrus provides frictionless data capture across all digital channels and devices, ensures compliance by design, and ultimately makes digital data instantly usable wherever required. We thrive on solving complex digital data challenges to help businesses succeed.

Celebrus Technologies Plc is a global business operating in over 30 countries today. We are quoted on the AIM Market of The London Stock Exchange (CLBS).

For more information, please see www.celebrus.com .

data.title: EU to bring forward review of 2035 vehicle emissions targets
data.description:
The European Commission will bring forward a review of its 2035 CO2 emission targets to the end of this year from 2026, EU sources said on Friday after the bloc's executive chief hosted auto executives to discuss the industry's future.

The EU has set a target of a 100% reduction of CO2 emissions for new cars and vans by 2035, which auto sector groups have said is no longer feasible. The target has been taken to mean the end of the internal combustion engine in new vehicles.

The review will take a specific look at vans, a member of Commission Executive Vice-President Stephane Sejourne's team said. Electric vans have a market share of only 8.5% of all new vans sold in the EU, about half the share for electric cars.

The details of a new proposal on the 2035 target are yet not clear, but it could include CO2 neutral fuels, such as biofuels, that could continue to power internal combustion engines, plug-in hybrids or range extenders.

The Commission will also present a proposal to establish a new regulatory category for small electric cars, which might benefit from lower tax treatment and contribute more towards meeting CO2 emission reduction targets

data.title: Candelaria Provides Update on MCTO
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, B.C.,September 12, 2025 – TheNewswire - Candelaria Mining Corp.(TSX-V: CAND.H, OTC PINK: CDELF) (“Candelaria” or the “Company”), is providing an update to its previously disclosed managementcease trade order ("MCTO"), announced on August 29, 2025, inrespect of the audited annual financial statements and correspondingmanagement's discussion and analysis for the year ended April 30,2025, including the CEO and CFO certifications (collectively, the"Annual Financial Filings") that were not filed by therequired filing deadline of August 28, 2025 (the "FilingDeadline").

As previously disclosed, the AnnualFinancial Filings were not filed by the Filing Deadline due to thefact that the Company’s auditors require additional time to completetheir audit and as a result, challenges with updating the Company’saccounts following the completion of the disposition of certainMexican subsidiaries by the Company.

The Company is working expeditiouslyon the steps required to complete the Annual Financial Filings andexpects to be able to file the Annual Financial Filings by September28, 2025. The Company will provide updates as further informationrelating to the Annual Financial Filings becomes available.

Until the Annual Financial Filingsare filed, the MCTO will be in effect. Additionally, the Company willissue bi-weekly default status reports in accordance with NationalPolicy 12-203 - Management Cease Trade Orders. The Company intends tosatisfy the provisions of the Alternative Intonation Guidelines duringthe period it remains in default of the filing requirements. TheCompany confirms that there have been no material businessdevelopments or other material information relating to its affairs asof the date of this news release that has not been generallydisclosed.

On Behalf ofCandelaria

Héctor Félix González Ramirez 

CEO and Director

For further information, pleasecontact:  

Omar García Abrego

CFO

+1 (604) 369-2928 | info@candelariamining.com

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

This news release includes certain “forward-looking statements”under applicable Canadian securities legislation. Forward lookingstatements are necessarily based upon a number of estimates andassumptions that, while considered reasonable, are subject to knownand unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors which may causethe actual results and future events to differ materially from thoseexpressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. Allstatements that address future plans, activities, events ordevelopments that the Company believes, expects or anticipates will ormay occur including the Company’s anticipated pipeline and value ofcurrent and customer deployments and future opportunities are themanagement’s best estimates and cannot be guaranteed or relied uponand is forward-looking information. There can be no assurance thatsuch statements will prove to be accurate, as actual results andfuture events could differ materially from those anticipated in suchstatements. Accordingly, readers should not place undue reliance onforward-looking statements. The Company disclaims any intention orobligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements in thisnews release, whether as a result of new information, future events orotherwise, except as required by law.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider(as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange)accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this pressrelease.

data.title: Deckers Outdoor Is Maintained at Overweight by Barclays
VINCE HOLDING CORP. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Vince Holding Corp., a leading contemporary fashion brand, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and business activities that have shaped the quarter.

Financial Highlights

Vince Holding Corp. reported the following financial results for the second quarter of 2025:

  • Net sales: $73.2 million, a decrease of 1.3% compared to the same period last year.
  • Gross profit: $36.9 million, representing 50.4% of net sales, an increase from 47.4% in the prior year due to lower product costing and higher pricing.
  • Income from operations: $11.2 million, or 15.2% of net sales, compared to $1.1 million, or 1.5% of net sales, in the prior year period.
  • Net income: $12.1 million, or 16.5% of net sales, compared to $0.6 million, or 0.8% of net sales, in the prior year period.
  • Basic earnings per share: $0.93, compared to $0.05 in the prior year period.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $0.93, compared to $0.05 in the prior year period.

Business Highlights

In addition to its financial performance, Vince Holding Corp. provided insights into its business operations for the quarter:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates two main segments: Vince Wholesale and Vince Direct-to-consumer. The Vince Wholesale segment focuses on distributing Vince brand products to major department stores and specialty stores in the U.S. and select international markets. The Vince Direct-to-consumer segment distributes products directly to consumers through full-price specialty retail stores, outlet stores, and the e-commerce platform.
  • Geographical Performance: The company reaches its customers through major wholesale department stores and specialty stores in the United States and select international markets, as well as through branded retail locations and the company's website. The company designs products in the U.S. and sources the majority of products from contract manufacturers outside the U.S., primarily in Asia.
  • Sales Units: As of August 2, 2025, the company operates 44 full-price retail stores and 14 outlet stores, in addition to its e-commerce site, vince.com.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that its future financial results may be subject to substantial fluctuations due to business conditions and macroeconomic factors, including tariffs and trade policies. The company expects to meet its liquidity needs through a combination of tariff mitigating initiatives, managing operating obligations, and ensuring partners satisfy their payment obligations.

SEC Filing: VINCE HOLDING CORP. [ VNCE ] - 10-Q - Sep. 12, 2025
